title: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office
display_title: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office
wikitext:
[]: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Joseki » Mon Mar 08, 2021 11:28 am https://blog.screenweek.it/2021/03/box- ... 70133.php/ 740 million yen for Shin's first day at the box office, beating Q's fist day record of 650m yen. The final numbers will be released tomorrow but I'm sure it could have performed MUCH WORSE considering the current situation. EDIT: I hope this isn't being discussed in the Spoiler hell already

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 08, 2021 11:32 am Joseki wrote: EDIT: I hope this isn't being discussed in the Spoiler hell already

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Mar 08, 2021 12:02 pm Excellent to hear! I was worried the box office would be hurt by Covid, but that doesn't appear to be the case. On the other hand, sure hope this doesn't cause another spike in cases.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Mon Mar 08, 2021 12:36 pm The numbers are amazing considering: - The seating limitations. - The reduced showtimes in four prefectures. (Which also unfortunately happen to be where the core fanbases are) - The 155 minutes runtime, means lesser showtimes in a day compared to other shorter films. Still, the insane number of showtimes this film has in a single theater and the extremely positive word-of-mouth would give the film a great leg at the weekly B.O. as there's no other major releases coming until next month (Conan movie). It may not surpass Demon Slayer but hey, at least it's still a highly successful one both critically and financially.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 08, 2021 2:35 pm How much is actually due to the movie being good vs. because it's the finale of a popular culturally relevant franchise?/It's almost a literary classic already esp. in Japan. Everyone saw Doctor Who Series 11 there was a big rating spike, but despite all the goodwill ppl then hated it so much no one tuned in for series 12.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by hui43210 » Mon Mar 08, 2021 2:43 pm Kendrix wrote: How much is actually due to the movie being good vs. because it's the finale of a popular culturally relevant franchise?/It's almost a literary classic already esp. in Japan. Everyone saw Doctor Who Series 11 there was a big rating spike, but despite all the goodwill ppl then hated it so much no one tuned in for series 12.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Mon Mar 08, 2021 3:46 pm By reading the audience reviews on sites like Eiga or Filmarks, it really seems like most of them think it's a "genuinely good" and "satisfying" film, compared to first day reviews of 3.0 which had tons of 1-2 star reviews and complaining they don't get the story and "feel", yet it's still successful because of the big name and curiosity factors. Now 3.0 remains as the lowest-rated film in the franchise on most Japanese film sites. I know, I know, some might disagree and while I'm in a love/hate relationship with 3.0, this is a general opinion from the audience. So yeah, I'm curious about how it'll perform for the rest of its theater lifetime with such strong first day reactions, but I don't think we'll see a massive dip in consensus rating (like from 4.5 to 2) unless the initial reviews gave people overtly high expectations and they got disappointed when they finally see it. EDIT: Found this photo of Grand Cinema Sunshine from yesterday morning (6:30am), it sure as hell crowded. I hope there are no spikes in cases because of this.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Mar 08, 2021 4:00 pm I mean lots of ppl were butthurt with EoE or indeed the last two eps too. Q was amazingly bold & artistically ambitious, as much as it hurt my feelings. XDD

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by baldur » Mon Mar 08, 2021 8:12 pm GhostlyOcam wrote: By reading the audience reviews on sites like Eiga or Filmarks, it really seems like most of them think it's a "genuinely good" and "satisfying" film, compared to first day reviews of 3.0 which had tons of 1-2 star reviews and complaining they don't get the story and "feel"

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Tajai » Mon Mar 08, 2021 8:17 pm Joseki wrote: https://blog.screenweek.it/2021/03/box-office-evangelion-apertura-record-in-giappone-di-%EF%BF%A5740m-minari-in-testa-in-corea-770133.php/ 740 million yen for Shin's first day at the box office, beating Q's fist day record of 650m yen. The final numbers will be released tomorrow but I'm sure it could have performed MUCH WORSE considering the current situation. EDIT: I hope this isn't being discussed in the Spoiler hell already

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Mon Mar 08, 2021 9:27 pm baldur wrote: Audience reactions being overwhelmingly positive feels, paradoxically enough, like a bad omen to me lmao

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by melonpan » Tue Mar 09, 2021 1:13 am Day 1 Box office results from Khara tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) And then here's an article on eva-info https://www.eva-info.jp/13137 EDIT: deleted some of my comments because it turns out I forgot how to read numbers in Japanese I seriously wonder how it's revenue will look by the time it's Wednesday. The limited seating and lack of screenings will only mean the money will pour in slower than in normal circumstances. I wonder how its day 1 would have done if things were normal or better covid wise

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Mar 09, 2021 5:26 am Evangelion: 3.0+1.0: Thrice Upon A Time (Shin Evangelion Gekijō-ban : | |), the "final" Evangelion film, sold over 530,000 tickets for over 800 million yen (about US$7.30 million) on its first day in 466 theaters on Monday. The film sold 21.7% more tickets and earned 23.8% more in yen on its opening day compared to the opening day of the previous Evangelion: 3.0 You Can (Not) Redo film in 2012. Evangelion: 3.0 opened on a Saturday. while Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 opened on a Monday.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 09, 2021 5:39 am I'll reserve my own applause until someone somewhere makes it some language I can comprehend so I can actually see the darn thing but at least the Search For More Money has been successful i guess

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Tue Mar 09, 2021 6:03 am Seems like the IMAX and 4DX screenings really helped a lot in getting extra money despite the lockdown still in effect in some area. More formats means more venues can play the film simultaneously and this also proves that despite the tickets being slightly more expensive, people don't really care anyway as long as they get to see it, it's also a rarity for prestige formats like those two to have big crowds as they tend to be some sort of "premium" options. Not to mention this is a Monday number while most blockbusters premiered on weekends! tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Mar 09, 2021 6:05 am The average ticket price increased yeah, from the article in the OP Il prezzo medio del biglietto dovrebbe essere sui ¥1,600, visto che sono stati esauriti quasi tutti gli spettacoli un IMAX e 4DX. Il prezzo medio di 3.0 nel 2012 era di circa ¥1,487 senza formati premium.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 09, 2021 7:07 am so they did a clever strategy then. Or ppl were more willing to buy fancy IMAX tickets since the regular cinemas could only use a fraction of the seats Well, where there's a will, there's a way, and a lot of ppl willed that movie in front of their eyeballs

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Mar 09, 2021 4:13 pm https://blog.screenweek.it/2021/03/box- ... 70316.php/ Day 1 records (official) - Best Monday ever - Best March debut ever - Best single-day ever in March Day 2 (preliminary) - 350 million yen (expected to fall gradually until Saturday) - 1.15 billion yen cumulative (projected to be the best 2 days in March ever) Long term projections - 5.5b/6b yen total (Q = 5.3b yen) - 4m tickets The article mentions repeted viewes are expected because the fanbase is very loyal.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 09, 2021 4:51 pm Joseki wrote: The article mentions repeted viewes are expected because the fanbase is very loyal.

[]: Re: Shin Evangelion beats franchise record for the first day at the box office             Quote Post by baldur » Tue Mar 09, 2021 6:26 pm GhostlyOcam wrote: I can tell you most people that went to the morning shows yesterday (therefore, the first ones who submitted the ratings/reviews) are hardcore/longtime fans who've been there from the beginning based on reviews I've read, if that's enough to assure you.
